gestures.mdmPrompt: A person steps slightly forward, opens arms wide, and smiles while warmly introducing the concept of addition.
The tutor stands tall, points to an imaginary number line in front, and explains the example clearly while engaging students.
The person shifts weight slightly, lifts left hand and counts to three using fingers while inviting students to respond.
The tutor leans slightly forward, extends right hand to count up to five, encouraging students to think through the problem.
The tutor stands confidently, raises both hands up slightly while emphasizing the importance of the final question to the class.
A person gently opens arms wide again, with a friendly smile, preparing to explain addition in a different way.
The tutor points towards an imaginary group of cars, enthusiastically outlining the new example as they explain it.
The person lightly sways from side to side, lifts left hand with fingers extended, counting as they engage students with the question.
The tutor confidently leans forward, raising the right hand to count aloud while prompting students to answer the next question.
The person stands firm, raising both hands slightly to reinforce the importance of the original question while looking around the class.
